title: Arachnophobia
review: A more fun and modern spider movie than {Kingdom of the Spiders}, with some cools sets and supporting roles. A problem that any kind of sneaky insect horror has, is that there's not really much you can do to add suspense, except have a creepy crawly creep out from behind some object in the scene. In most of this film, there are so many shots where a vase or curtain is in focus and placed weirdly centrally in the frame, while a human in the background isn't doing anything interesting You just know a spider is about to show up, and so you're not scared at all unless you have the titular condition. At least the opening section, and the big ending battle, are able to do something more memorable with the spiders. John Goodman's character in this is almost like something written by the Coen brothers, he adds a lot with only a couple of scenes.
